questions.question: 1 (a) Carry out the following instructions, referring to Fig. 2.1. • Connect the crocodile clip C to the free end of the resistance wire. • Switch on. Adjust the position of the crocodile clip C until the current shown on the ammeter is as near to 0.40 A as possible. • Measure, and record in Table 2.1, the poten

parts.answer: difficult to measure irregular dimensions / owtte repeat (in several places for each dimension and take averages)
ρ in range 1.3 to 2.3 unit g / cm

line of sight perpendicular; read to bottom of meniscus. ρ within 10% of ρ suggestion supported by valid reason e.g. ρ as volume is measured directly; ρ as measuring cylinder is less precise; string adds to volume displaced. 0625/53 October/November 2018

V values all increasing V all < 3.00 V and to at least 1 dp axes labelled correct orientation, with quantity and unit appropriate scales (plots occupying at least ½ grid) plots all correct to ½ small square and precise plots well-judged line and thin line G present and triangle method seen on graph l in range 10 to 20 (cm) R in range 0.05 to 0.11 / 3 sig figs and unit = Ω or Ω / cm one from: use smaller current / potential difference; use wire with greater resistance; open switch / switch off circuit in between readings. 0625/53 October/November 2018
P P pin separations ⩾ 5.0 cm line in correct place and neat θ = 40 ° ± °

statement matching results including qualitative justif ication (e.g. ‘within limits of experimental accuracy’ / owtte) justification that makes use of the data to support statement two suitable precautions: pins upright / view pins at base; thin pins; thin lines / sharp pencil; large pin separations; ensure mirrored surface lies along drawn line. 0625/53 October/November 2018 MP1 Apparatus thermometer AND stopwatch / (stop)clock / timer MP2 Diagram workable arrangement MP3 Method hot water in beaker and cold water in test-tube AND measure (start and) end temperatures in test-tube AND over measured time MP4 Method repeat for different metals MP5 Comparison calculation of (rate of) temperature rise / heating curves for different metals MP6 and MP7 Precautions Any two from: same start temperatures (of hot / cold water); same duration of experiment; same volume of cold water (being heated); repeat experiment and take average (of calculated values); use of insulation; stir (cold) water; heat to keep water in beaker at constant temperature.
